claim: Crawl4AI deployed via Docker as an API endpoint (2-5 min setup on Digital Ocean) outperforms built-in n8n web scraping tools in speed, ease of use, and cost (free/open-source)
actionable: Deploy Crawl4AI on Digital Ocean App Platform with basic-amd64 image; use its API endpoint in n8n instead of built-in HTTP scraping nodes
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code

claim: Sitemap.xml files provide efficient bulk URL discovery for scraping; most documentation and e-commerce sites expose them at domain/sitemap.xml for ethical crawling
actionable: Start scraping workflows by fetching and parsing sitemap.xml instead of manually enumerating URLs; check robots.txt and ToS first to ensure ethical scraping
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code

claim: Crawl4AI HTTP endpoints use async task IDs for scraping (not immediate returns) requiring poll-based status checking, necessitating wait loops until task completion
actionable: Call /crawl endpoint to get task ID, wait 5+ seconds, then poll /task/[ID] endpoint until status='completed' rather than expecting synchronous results
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code

claim: Document chunking at 5000 tokens is a reasonable default for RAG vector stores but should be tuned per use case; larger chunks work for dense documents, smaller for diverse queries
actionable: Start with 5000-token chunks in your RAG pipeline; A/B test smaller (2000) and larger (8000) sizes against your actual query patterns to optimize retrieval
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code

claim: Supabase vector store setup requires running provided SQL scripts to create documents table and match_documents() function, which most users must do once per project
actionable: Copy the Supabase quickstart SQL from Crawl4AI docs and execute it in your Supabase SQL editor before connecting n8n vector store node
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code

claim: Crawl4AI is CPU/RAM intensive due to headless browser usage; hosting it separately from n8n prevents performance degradation of main workflow engine
actionable: Host Crawl4AI on dedicated cloud instance separate from n8n; avoid localhost deployment in production to prevent resource contention
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code

claim: Ethical web scraping requires checking robots.txt and terms of service before bulk crawling; many sites explicitly prohibit scraping and legal/ethical obligations apply
actionable: Always check domain/robots.txt and legal docs before deploying any scraper; document which sites permit crawling and adjust batch sizes accordingly
source_title: n8n + Crawl4AI - Scrape ANY Website in Minutes with NO Code
